edges.description: Tumor-triggered immunity against intracellular neural antigens can generate cytotoxic T-cell-dominant neural injury.
edges.target: Cytotoxic T Cell-Mediated Neuronal Destruction (Intracellular Antigen PNS)

edges.description: Tumor-triggered immunity can also produce surface-antigen autoantibodies that perturb synaptic or neuronal signaling.
edges.target: Pathogenic Autoantibody-Mediated Synaptic Dysfunction (Surface Antigen PNS)

edges.description: Cytotoxic neural injury can cause irreversible focal or multifocal neurologic syndromes.
edges.source: Cytotoxic T Cell-Mediated Neuronal Destruction (Intracellular Antigen PNS)

edges.description: Surface-antigen autoantibody dysfunction can affect CNS, peripheral nerves, autonomic pathways, and neuromuscular signaling.
edges.source: Pathogenic Autoantibody-Mediated Synaptic Dysfunction (Surface Antigen PNS)

edges.target: Cytotoxic T Cell-Mediated Neuronal Destruction (Intracellular Antigen PNS)

nodes.description: Amifampridine, also called 3,4-diaminopyridine, is symptomatic therapy for Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome, a neuromuscular-junction PNS phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome: Epidemiology and therapeutic response in the national veterans affairs population.

evidence.snippet: Only the Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome is relatively frequent, occurring in about 1% of patients with small cell lung cancer.

nodes.description: Identifying and treating the underlying tumor is central because tumor removal or control can reduce the antigenic driver of PNS.

evidence.snippet: to date, the best way to stabilize PNS is to treat the cancer as soon as possible.

nodes.description: Corticosteroids are used as acute immunosuppressive therapy for autoimmune and paraneoplastic neurologic presentations, particularly when inflammatory CNS or cerebellar involvement is suspected.

evidence.reference_title: Responses to and Outcomes of Treatment of Autoimmune Cerebellar Ataxia in Adults.
evidence.snippet: Responses to immunotherapy (corticosteroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasma exchange, and immunosuppressants)

nodes.description: Intracellular-antigen PNS is dominated by cytotoxic T-cell effector biology against neurons expressing shared tumor-neural antigens. Onconeural antibodies such as anti-Hu or anti-Yo often mark this process, but the destructive injury is modeled through cytotoxic lymphocyte-mediated neuronal death.
nodes.id: Cytotoxic T Cell-Mediated Neuronal Destruction (Intracellular Antigen PNS)

evidence.reference_title: Paraneoplastic neurological autoimmunity and survival of the neuron: the role of the cancer immunity cycle.
evidence.snippet: Disorders accompanied by autoantibody markers of neural peptide-specific cytotoxic effector T cells

evidence.reference_title: Paraneoplastic neurologic syndrome associated with gynecologic and breast malignancies.
evidence.snippet: leading to the destruction of Purkinje cells harboring the Yo antigens

nodes.description: Immunotherapy may be used to suppress the immune process, with treatment choice and prognosis varying by syndrome, antibody target, and tumor control.

evidence.reference_title: Paraneoplastic neurological syndromes: clinical presentations and management
evidence.snippet: Specific management strategies and prognosis vary widely depending on the underlying etiology.
suppression of the immune response represents another treatment approach.

nodes.description: Intravenous immunoglobulin is used as antibody-directed or immune-modulating therapy in selected PNS phenotypes, including opsoclonus-myoclonus and autoimmune cerebellar presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Responses to and Outcomes of Treatment of Autoimmune Cerebellar Ataxia in Adults.
evidence.snippet: Responses to immunotherapy (corticosteroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasma exchange, and immunosuppressants)

evidence.reference_title: CSF neurofilament light chain is elevated in OMS (decreasing with immunotherapy) and other pediatric neuroinflammatory disorders.
evidence.snippet: On combination treatment with front-loaded ACTH, IVIg, rituximab, median CSF NFL decreased by 60% to control levels.

nodes.description: PNS phenotypes vary by antibody, tumor, and neural target and may include rapidly progressive cerebellar syndrome, encephalitis, opsoclonus-myoclonus, sensory neuropathy, retinopathy, neuromuscular junction dysfunction, or gastrointestinal pseudo-obstruction.

evidence.snippet: The most common PNS are Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome (LEMS), subacute cerebellar ataxia, limbic encephalitis (LE), opsoclonus-myoclonus (OM), retinopathies

nodes.description: LEMS, dermatomyositis, and other neuromuscular PNS phenotypes can manifest with weakness.
nodes.id: Muscle Weakness from Neuromuscular Junction or Muscle Involvement

evidence.snippet: Only the Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome is relatively frequent, occurring in about 1% of patients with small cell lung cancer.

nodes.description: Surface-antigen PNS involves plasma-membrane or synaptic autoantibodies that can disrupt neuronal communication. These antibody-mediated syndromes are modeled separately from intracellular-antigen disease because the effector mechanism is more directly humoral and often more treatment-responsive.
nodes.id: Pathogenic Autoantibody-Mediated Synaptic Dysfunction (Surface Antigen PNS)

evidence.reference_title: Paraneoplastic neurological autoimmunity and survival of the neuron: the role of the cancer immunity cycle.
evidence.snippet: Disorders accompanied by neural plasma membrane-reactive autoantibodies

evidence.reference_title: Paraneoplastic Neurological Syndromes: Advances and Future Perspectives in Immunopathogenesis and Management.
evidence.snippet: antibody-mediated disorders targeting neuronal surface proteins

nodes.description: Plasma exchange is used to reduce circulating pathogenic antibody burden in antibody-mediated PNS or severe autoimmune neurologic presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Responses to and Outcomes of Treatment of Autoimmune Cerebellar Ataxia in Adults.
evidence.snippet: Responses to immunotherapy (corticosteroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasma exchange, and immunosuppressants)

nodes.description: Rituximab is an anti-CD20 B-cell-depleting therapy used in selected antibody-mediated or refractory PNS phenotypes, especially when ongoing humoral autoimmunity is suspected.

evidence.reference_title: CSF neurofilament light chain is elevated in OMS (decreasing with immunotherapy) and other pediatric neuroinflammatory disorders.
evidence.snippet: On combination treatment with front-loaded ACTH, IVIg, rituximab, median CSF NFL decreased by 60% to control levels.

nodes.description: A tumor expressing antigens shared with neural tissue can trigger adaptive immunity against tumor cells that cross-recognizes the nervous system.

biological_processes: antigen processing and presentation of peptide antigen via MHC class I

evidence.snippet: PNS are caused by autoimmune processes triggered by the cancer and directed against antigens common to both the cancer and the nervous system
